name: Basic Pizza Dough #1
recipeIngredient: 4 tbsp Olive oil or vegetable oil
recipeInstructions:
1. Add water, olive oil (or vegetable oil), salt, bread flour, and yeast to the bread machine pan in the order suggested by the manufacturer.
2. Select dough/manual cycle.
3. When the cycle is complete, remove the dough from the machine to a lightly floured surface. If necessary, knead in enough flour to make the dough easy to handle.
4. Preheat oven to 425°F (220°C).
5. For each pizza, grease the pan (see tip below for pan size) and sprinkle with cornmeal.
6. Roll out the dough and place on the pan or pat it in the pan.
7. Top each pizza with 1/2 to 3/4 cup sauce, 1/2 to 3/4 pound cooked meat or 3 1/2 to 5 ounces pepperoni, 1/2 to 1 cup sliced or chopped onion, green bell pepper and/or other vegetables, and 1 to 1 1/2 cups shredded cheese.
8. Bake for 15 to 25 minutes or until done - pizza is done when edges of crust are golden and cheese is bubbly.
9. Yield: 1 or 2 pizzas.
Tip: For a 1-pound recipe, use a 12-inch round pan or a 13-by-9-inch pan for a thick crust; a 14-inch round pan for a thin crust. For a 1 1/2-pound recipe, use a 14-inch round pan for a thick crust, two 12-inch round pans, or one 15-by-10-inch baking sheet for a thin crust.
Variations:
- Whole-wheat pizza: Substitute whole-wheat flour for half of the bread flour.
- Basil-Parmesan pizza: Add 3 tbsp (1/4 cup) grated Parmesan cheese and 3/4 tsp (1 tsp) dried basil to the machine pan with flour. Top with green bell pepper, red onion, tomato, and mozzarella cheese.
- Taco pizza: Add 2 tbsp (3 tbsp) cornmeal and 1 tbsp (4 tsp) taco seasoning mix with the flour; omit the salt. Top with taco sauce, cooked ground beef, onion, green bell pepper, Cheddar cheese. Add shredded lettuce and chopped tomato just before serving.
